title: Calendar Shelf Command Center
summary: A wall-hung family command center: two dry-erase calendars framed by ledge shelves, key hooks, and a mail basket on one board panel.
description:
A wall organizer that gathers the whole family's logistics onto one panel: two dry-erase month calendars, a couple of ledge shelves to prop cards or photos, a row of cup hooks for keys, and a mail basket. It's built from three 1x6 boards laid up as a backing panel with 1x2 ledges — a quick, high-impact build that solves the pile of mail and lost keys by the door.

There's almost no joinery. The two ledge shelves are just 1x2s nailed into an L, and everything mounts to the 1x6 panel with brad nails and glue. The store-bought pieces — the calendars and the mail basket — set your layout, so have them in hand and dry-lay the whole arrangement on the panel before you commit any nails.

The one thing that matters for safety is the hanger. Loaded with mail and keys this panel isn't light, so hang it from a wall stud or use a rated 30-pound picture hanger. A command center that pulls off the wall onto the floor defeats the purpose.

tools.note: Predrill for the cup hooks and mount the hanger.

materials.name: 1x6, 4 ft
materials.note: 12 board feet total — the backing panel.

steps.title: Build the ledge shelves
steps.body: Nail the 1x2s together into L-shaped ledge shelves — a base with a small lip to keep cards from sliding off. Build two.

steps.title: Lay out and attach everything to the panel
steps.body: Lay the three 1x6 boards side by side as your panel. Dry-lay the calendars, the ledge shelves, and the key-hook base on the panel and shuffle until the arrangement works, then attach the shelves and hook base with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. Predrill and thread in the cup hooks along the hook base.

steps.title: Add the basket, finish, and hang
steps.body: Paint or stain the panel, then add the mail basket and the calendars. Attach the heavy-duty hanging kit and hang it to a wall stud or with a 30-pound-rated hanger — loaded up, this panel has real weight.

images.alt: Wall-hung family command center with two calendars, ledge shelves, key hooks, and a mail basket
